paper_topic: NIH grant termination patterns in 2025 — training vs R&D grants and role of institutional type
primary_result_context: OR = 1.11 (95% CI: 0.90–1.36) training vs R&D; Hospital/NonProfit institutions OR ~10.5 vs University-Medical
data_source: NIH Grant Witness termination dataset (grantwitness.com), n=5,219 grants

search_strategy.api_status: heavily rate-limited — S2 returned limited results; supplement with verified @misc entries

entries_excluded.reason: Irrelevant — heart failure clinical trial, not NIH funding or methods
Partially relevant — RECORD statement for pharmacoepidemiology, superseded by STROBE-Equity and Langan2018 already included
Marginally relevant — Latin America biomedical capacity, not directly about NIH US termination patterns
Duplicate — same article as Dewidar2025, different journal (simultaneous JAMA Network Open / BMJ publication)

Classic STROBE methods paper (PMID 17938396) — well-known, search confirmed relevance; manually added as API did not return it directly
